title: Viola × multifida
intro: Viola × multifida, also known as the variable pansy, is a hybrid plant species within the genus Viola, characterized by its unique, variable foliage and vibrant flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Viola × multifida include flavonoids, anthocyanins, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Viola × multifida include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to harness its health benefits and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Viola × multifida is a hybrid of the genus Viola, which is a group of small, delicate flowering plants commonly known as pansies or violas. These plants are members of the Violaceae family. This classification places Viola × multifida within the larger plant kingdom, specifically in the division Magnoliophyta and the class Magnoliopsida. The family Violaceae is further grouped under the order Malpighiales and the clade Rosidae. The exact classification of Viola × multifida can be considered as part of the broader classification of flowering plants (angiosperms).
uses: Viola × multifida is reportedly used in traditional medicine for its potential antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. It is said to have been used to treat various ailments such as skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues. The plant's roots and leaves are believed to contain bioactive compounds with potential medicinal value. However, more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human use. Traditional healers in certain regions have used Viola × multifida for centuries, suggesting its potential medicinal properties.
benefits: Viola × multifida, also known as the heartleaf violet or multifid violet, has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of digestive problems and skin conditions. The plant's leaves and flowers are thought to be rich in antioxidants, which can help protect the body from oxidative stress and cell damage. Additionally, Viola × multifida is said to have antiseptic and antiviral properties, making it a potential remedy for wounds and infections. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Viola × multifida for medicinal purposes.

constituents: Viola × multifida contains active constituents such as iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which may contribute to its medicinal properties. These compounds have been reported to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. The iridoid glycosides in Viola × multifida may also exhibit cardiotonic and vasodilatory effects. Additionally, the plant's flavonoids have been shown to have potential anti-cancer and neuroprotective properties. Further research is needed to fully understand the pharmacological effects of Viola × multifida's active constituents.
